content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 17, 1992


Diana Joyce Mitchell
847 Marina Drive
Napa, CA 94559

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-92-340

Dear Ms. Mitchell:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you on June 4, 1992 concerning Section 84308 of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act") and the provisions of the Act that
regulate political campaigns.

This letter confirms that your letter dated June 10, 1992,
accurately summarizes the application of the Act to the facts you
have provided. You stated that you are a Napa County Planning
Commissioner, and you were also a candidate for Napa County
Supervisor in 1992. On June 2, 1992, you lost the election to the
County Board of Supervisors and ended the campaign with a debt
remaining. You asked what restrictions applied to your
fundraising to extinguish the campaign debts.

You were advised that you may continue to raise funds to pay
the campaign debt. However, so long as you continue to raise
funds, you are required to retain the campaign account and file
the required periodic disclosures. (Sections 85200 and 85201;
Section 84214.) You were also advised that you may repay a
personal loan you made to your committee which has been properly
reported pursuant to the disclosure provisions of the Act.
(Section 84211 and 84216; Section 89512.)

In addition, you were advised that as a planning commissioner
you were subject to Section 84308. Section 84308 prohibits
officers of appointed boards or commissions from accepting,
soliciting, or directing a contribution of more than $250 from any

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-92-340
Page 2


party or participant² in a proceeding involving a license, permit,
or other entitlement for use³ while the matter is pending before
the agency and for three months following the date of a final
decision. (Section 84308(b).)

You stated that an applicant appeared before the planning
commission on May 6, 1992. Thus, you may not accept, solicit, or
direct a contribution of more than $250 from that applicant for
three months after May 6, 1992. As we discussed, contributors who
make contributions of $250 or less will not be subject to the
prohibition in section 84308.

Please note, Section 84308 also requires disclosure and
disqualification if you have accepted a contribution of more than
$250 during the 12 months prior to a decision. (Section 84308(c);
Regulation 18438.8.) Thus, once you accept a contribution of more
than $250 from a person, you will be subject to the disclosure and
disqualification provisions for 12 months. Moreover, the section
prohibits the acceptance of a contribution of more than $250 from
an applicant while the proceeding is pending before your agency.

I trust that this answers your questions. If you have any
further questions regarding this matter, please feel free to
contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Luisa Menchaca
     Counsel, Legal Division

SH:LM:aa

2  "Participant" means any person who is not a party but who
actively supports or opposes a particular decision in a proceeding
involving a license, permit, or other entitlement for use and who
has a financial interest in the decision. (Section 84308(a)(2);
Section 87103.) Section 84308 is applicable regarding
participants only if the officer knows or has reason to know of
the participant's financial interest. (Steiner Advice Letter, No.
A-83-080.)

³ "License, permit, or other entitlement for use" means all
business, professional, trade and land use licenses and permits
and all other entitlements for use, including all entitlements for
land use, all contracts (other than competitively bid, labor, or
personal employment contracts), and all franchises. (Section
84308(a)(5).)

sections.question_synthetic: What restrictions apply to fundraising for campaign debts after losing an election, and what contribution limits apply to a planning commissioner under Section 84308?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The requestor may continue raising funds to pay campaign debts while maintaining the campaign account and filing required disclosures. As a planning commissioner, she cannot accept contributions over $250 from parties or participants in proceedings before the agency while pending and for three months after final decision, and must disclose/disqualify herself if accepting contributions over $250 from applicants within 12 months of a decision.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No explicit QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers, but document structure clearly identifies it as FPPC response confirming prior telephone advice. Content extracted from body paragraphs describing the advised restrictions and requirements.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What restrictions apply to fundraising for campaign debts after losing an election, and what contribution limits apply to a planning commissioner under Section 84308?

CONCLUSION: The requestor may continue raising funds to pay campaign debts while maintaining the campaign account and filing required disclosures. As a planning commissioner, she cannot accept contributions over $250 from parties or participants in proceedings before the agency while pending and for three months after final decision, and must disclose/disqualify herself if accepting contributions over $250 from applicants within 12 months of a decision.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms telephone advice given to a Napa County Planning Commissioner and 1992 County Supervisor candidate regarding campaign debt fundraising requirements and Section 84308 contribution restrictions applicable to appointed board officers.
